name: Hani ibn Urwa
title: Host of the Ambassador
role: Host of Muslim and Martyr of Kufa
shortDescription: A prominent elder of Kufa and chief of the Murad tribe who sacrificed his life and wealth to shelter the Imam's ambassador.
fullStory:
Hani ibn Urwa al-Muradi was one of the greatest elders and tribal leaders of Kufa; with a single call, thousands of swordsmen from the Madhhij tribe would come to his aid. He was a sincere Shia and loyal companion of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and fought in the battles of Jamal, Siffin, and Nahrawan. When Muslim ibn Aqeel arrived in Kufa as Imam Hussain's (AS) ambassador and felt endangered, he sought refuge in Hani's secure and powerful house.

Hosting the Imam's representative in that oppressive environment was a massive risk, but Hani gladly and bravely accepted this honor. Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad, who had learned of Muslim's hiding place, deceitfully lured Hani to the governor's palace. Ibn Ziyad demanded that he surrender Muslim, but Hani, with his Arab zeal and Islamic honor, stood firm and said: 'By God, I will never surrender my guest to you until I am killed'.

Enraged by this resistance, Ibn Ziyad struck Hani's face with his cane so hard that it broke his nose and forehead. Hani, barehanded, attacked one of the guards to take his sword but was unsuccessful and was imprisoned by Ibn Ziyad's order. The news of his arrest led to Muslim's brief uprising and the subsequent betrayal by the Kufans.

After the martyrdom of Muslim ibn Aqeel, Ubaydullah ordered Hani to be taken to the sheep market of Kufa to be beheaded. On the way to the market, Hani loudly called his tribe (Madhhij) for help, but no one dared to assist him out of fear of Ibn Ziyad.

Finally, on the 8th of Dhu al-Hijjah (the very day Imam Hussain left Mecca), Hani ibn Urwa was martyred with his hands tied but his head held high, by a Turkish slave named Rashid. His body and that of Muslim ibn Aqeel were dragged through the streets of Kufa to serve as a warning to the disloyal Kufans.
quoteArabic: وَاللَّهِ لا أَدْفَعُهُ إِلَيْكَ أَبَداً، أَجيئُكَ بِضَيْفي وَرَسولِ حُسَيْنٍ لِتَقْتُلَهُ؟
quote: By God, I will never surrender him to you; shall I bring you my guest for you to kill?
